title: lemon cake
description: A classic lemon cake from Le Manoir, delicious for afternoon tea.
context: Pâtisserie course 2012. Raymond Blanc Cookery School. Introduction notes that this is a real favourite at Le Manoir. Planning ahead: You can make the glaze a day in advance.
steps: In a large bowl whisk together the eggs, sugar, cream and lemon zest.
Separately sieve together the flour and baking powder twice, then add to the bowl followed by the rum and salt, melted butter and oil. Whisk until smooth.
Grease your loaf tin with a little softened butter and line with greaseproof paper.
Fill the tin with the mixture two thirds full and bake for 50–60 minutes, turning halfway through cooking.
To check if it is cooked insert a small knife blade into the middle of the cake; if it comes out clean it is done.
Turn it out onto a baking rack and leave to cool.
Lightly brush the cake all over with the hot apricot jelly.
In a small saucepan gently warm the lemon juice and lemon zest and mix with the icing sugar to form a white, cloudy glaze.
Brush the glaze over the cake and leave for a few minutes to set before slicing.
For a smoother finish, return the glazed cake to the oven for a few minutes to gently warm and smooth out any lumps in the icing.
